title: Missing #[must_use] on error types and Result-returning functions
description:
File: rust/lichen-ipv6/src/lib.rs

Several functions return Result but lack #[must_use]:
- Ipv6Header::write_to() (line 288)
- Ipv6Header::from_bytes() (line 315)
- UdpHeader::write_to() (line 372)
- UdpHeader::from_bytes() (line 401)
- Icmpv6Echo::build_request/build_reply (lines 429, 441)
- NeighborSolicitation::from_bytes (line 538)
- parse_packet (line 706)
- handle_icmpv6 (line 722)

Without #[must_use], callers can accidentally ignore the Result, missing errors silently.

Error types also lack #[must_use]:
- TooShort (line 25)
- BufferTooSmall (line 54)
- Ipv6Error (line 84)

Fix: Add #[must_use] attribute to all Result-returning functions and error types:
```rust
#[must_use]
pub fn from_bytes(buf: &[u8]) -> Result<Self, Ipv6Error> { ... }

#[must_use]
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub struct TooShort { ... }
```
